episode_summary: A tongue‑in‑cheek “worst of 2014” compilation introduced by Alex Bell gathers the gang’s strangest tidbits, from sports stats and haunted body parts to animal biology and Victorian tech oddities. The episode ricochets through quirky anecdotes, historical curios, and science snippets from across the year.

facts.fact: It's really tiny. There's only average per match, 11 minutes of movement.

There was a guy in the medical literature who had a haunted scrotum and he went to the doctor because he had an undecided right testicle. And when they did a scan on him, they saw his left testicle and they said the left side of his scrotum seemed to be occupied by a screaming ghost-like apparition.
So for alligators, if the egg is kept in a nest at 30 degrees, it turns out female. And if it's 34 degrees, it turns out male.
In 1860, a new technology entered European life. Women who used it complained that it caused extreme genital excitement.
